outcome: Motion approved unanimously; question placed on June 18, 2019 Special Election ballot.

what_was_tried: Approved a year-end transfer of $350,000 from the Town to the Schools to address the FY19 School Department deficit, driven in part by growth in out-of-district Special Education placements and FY18 SPED bills paid in FY19.
outcome: Motion passed unanimously; transfer also requires Finance Committee approval.

pass2_notes: Substantive discussion and unanimous vote on $350K mid-year transfer to address school deficit driven partly by SPED costs; notable policy moment with new bill-checking procedures instituted.

what_was_tried: Town Administrator issued a budget message directing all department heads to submit a level-funded budget plus a 10% reduced budget option, and to conduct a fee analysis benchmarked against comparable communities, in response to the projected FY20 deficit of over $1,000,000.
outcome: Town Administrator reported productive individual meetings with department heads; exercise underway to identify potential reductions and revenue adjustments.
evidence_quote: In light of this situation the Town Administrator sent out a budget message to all Department Heads that outlines his request for each department to submit a level funded budget as well as a 10% reduced budget and to review/conduct a fee analyses in their department and look at how they compare with local communities.
minutes_section: Budget: School Department
pass2_notes: Town-wide directive for level-funded and 10% reduced budgets plus fee analysis in response to >$1M projected FY20 deficit; substantive systemic response.

what_was_tried: Engaged Powers and Sullivan to conduct an investigation and audit of the practice of holding FY18 Special Education bills and paying them in FY19, and instituted new procedures requiring all school bills coming to the accounting office to be double-checked by the Finance Director.
outcome: Audit completed confirming the practice; new double-check procedure implemented in the Finance Director's office.
evidence_quote: Once Town management became aware of this situation the Town engaged Powers and Sullivan to conduct an investigation and audit to allow the Town to have a complete understanding of what happened and assistance in fully quantifying the problem financially.
minutes_section: Budget: School Department
pass2_notes: Major fiscal oversight failure: FY18 Special Ed bills paid in FY19 violating state law; triggered forensic audit, new double-check procedures, and $350K year-end transfer vote.

what_was_tried: Adopted a Tax Title Policy dedicating not less than 10% of sale proceeds from tax title foreclosure dispositions, in the following fiscal year, to the Affordable Housing Trust Fund upon Town Meeting approval, to support affordable housing programs.
outcome: Motion passed, all in favor.
evidence_quote: Whenever property disposition through tax title foreclosure is conducted the Town of Marblehead shall dedicate an amount not less than 10% of the sale proceeds, in the following fiscal year, for the purpose of supporting affordable housing and affordable housing programs. Upon approval of Town Meeting, the funds shall be deposited in the town's Affordable Housing Trust Fund.
minutes_section: Tax Title Policy
pass2_notes: Adopted policy dedicating ≥10% of tax title foreclosure proceeds to Affordable Housing Trust Fund — substantive, systemic housing action via vote.
